query: What is Elon Musk's current net worth according to Bloomberg Billionaires Index in early July 2026? Has it recovered above $1 trillion after the late June 2026 tech selloff? Include recent Tesla and SpaceX share price and valuation trajectory.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

**Elon Musk's net worth in early July 2026:** ~**$932 billion** per the Bloomberg Billionaires Index (and ~$916B per Forbes) — meaning he has **NOT durably recovered above $1 trillion** after the late-June 2026 tech selloff. His fortune is oscillating right around the trillion-dollar line, mostly below it, though some real-time retail trackers show ~$1.04T (July 10).

**Key trajectory:**
- **June 12:** SpaceX's record IPO made Musk the first-ever trillionaire (~$1.1T)
- **June 16:** Peaked at an all-time high ~$1.45T (SpaceX hit $225.64/share)
- **June 22–24:** Tech selloff crashed SpaceX 16.4% (to <$155, −31% from peak) + $116B Tesla share restrictions → stripped him of trillionaire status, a $300–350B loss
- **Early July:** Fortune hovering just under $1T on Bloomberg

**Tesla (TSLA):** $407.76 (July 10), ~$1.53T market cap, ~18% below 52-week high; recovered from a June 25 low of $375.12.

**SpaceX (SPCX):** IPO priced $135, briefly valued >$2.1T; peaked $225.64 (June 16), then crashed to <$155. Musk's ~42% stake makes SpaceX the primary driver of his net-worth volatility.

The full structured findings (key facts, 10 sources, 18 quantitative data points) have been submitted.

key_facts: Bloomberg Billionaires Index lists Elon Musk at ~$932 billion 'as of July 2026'; Forbes lists ~$916 billion — both below $1 trillion.
Musk has NOT durably recovered above $1 trillion; his net worth has frequently dropped under $1T since late June 2026, though some real-time trackers show ~$1.04T (July 10).
SpaceX's June 12, 2026 IPO (priced $135/share, closed ~$160) made Musk the first trillionaire in recorded history (~$1.1T).
Musk's net worth peaked at an all-time high of ~$1.45 trillion around June 16, 2026 when SpaceX hit ~$225.64/share.
A late-June 2026 tech selloff crashed SpaceX 16.4% on June 22 to under $155 (~31% below its June 16 peak), erasing $300-350B and stripping Musk of trillionaire status by June 24.
New restrictions on ~$116 billion of Musk's Tesla shares also contributed to his fall below $1 trillion.
Tesla (TSLA) traded at $407.76 on July 10, 2026 with a ~$1.53 trillion market cap, ~18% below its 52-week high of $498.83.
TSLA hit a recent low of $375.12 on June 25, 2026, then rebounded ~13% into the low-$400s by mid-July.
Musk owns ~42% of SpaceX and ~20% of Tesla; SpaceX is the primary driver of his net worth volatility.
SpaceX's IPO valuation briefly topped $2.1 trillion intraday on June 12, the largest IPO in history, briefly surpassing Amazon in value.
Musk holds unvested compensation ($1 trillion Tesla stock package plus additional SpaceX stock tied to milestones) not counted in current net worth.
Musk remains the world's richest person by a wide margin, with Larry Page a distant second (~$296B per Fox Business).
